world-news.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
world-news.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.

world-news.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
world-news.summary: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

world-news.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
world-news.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear.

world-news.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
world-news.summary: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

world-news.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
world-news.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

world-news.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
world-news.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence".

world-news.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
world-news.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.

world-news.title: Teen jailed for exam cheating in Guinea dies after sickle cell complications, father says
world-news.summary: Mamadou Djouma Bah, 17, was arrested and sentenced to prison for exam fraud earlier this month.

world-news.title: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
world-news.summary: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.

world-news.title: Nicaragua's legislature debates barring opposition from elections
world-news.summary: The move comes days after President Daniel Ortega demanded 'a wall, a blockade' against opposition parties.

tech-ai.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
tech-ai.summary: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

tech-ai.title: Meet the judges who will crown Australia’s next breakout startup
tech-ai.summary: TechCrunch Startup Battlefield is coming to Australia — and we're partnering with Stripe to find the country's most exciting early-stage startups.

tech-ai.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
tech-ai.summary: Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat.

tech-ai.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
tech-ai.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

tech-ai.title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce
tech-ai.summary: In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable.

tech-ai.title: Insurance startup Corgi reportedly raised more money at $4B — its third round in 8 weeks
tech-ai.summary: In the AI-funding frenzy, many startups are raising back-to-back rounds at ever-increasing valuations — but even by those standards, Corgi stands out.

tech-ai.title: Tesla’s door handles may spur new US safety rules
tech-ai.summary: The new rule-making process follows a series of incidents, including fatal ones, in which people have become stuck inside cars.

tech-ai.title: Meta drops out of a major clean energy pact as its natural gas buildout accelerates
tech-ai.summary: Meta has made significant investments in natural gas over the past year. Now it's dropping out of an industry renewable energy group.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
tech-ai.summary: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

tech-ai.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
tech-ai.summary: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Geely E5 review: a successful electric debut at a competitive price
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Geely has launched in Europe with its own brand presence with modern design and competent technology. The electric SUV E5 enters the popular but competitive compact segment. Whether the overall package works is shown in our first test drive.

automotive-ev-battery.title: CALB under pressure over GAC Aion S battery issues
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Aion S, a popular electric car in China, is said to be experiencing growing issues with LFP battery cells. The problems primarily affect vehicles with high mileage. Manufacturer GAC Aion and cell supplier CALB are responding with an extended warranty and additional service measures.

automotive-ev-battery.title: DSV orders 10 Windrose electric trucks for US-Mexico freight
automotive-ev-battery.summary: In partnership with transport company Allogic and charging infrastructure provider Greenspace E-Mobility, logistics firm DSV is set to deploy ten battery-electric Windrose heavy-duty trucks on the I-35 freight corridor. This marks the first commercial fleet deployment on Greenspace's planned 'Electric Highway' network linking the US and Mexico.

automotive-ev-battery.title: SEG Automotive joins Easelink’s Matrix Charging initiative
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Easelink and SEG Automotive plan to bring the automated charging system Matrix Charging into series production. In addition to initial OEM projects, SEG Automotive plans to integrate the technology into its own product portfolio on a license basis.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Greece tightens rules for e-scooters after crash surge
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Greece has introduced stricter rules for electric kick scooter use following a series of accidents. The new legislation mandates helmets and liability insurance, raises the minimum riding age to 17 and introduces higher fines for a range of traffic offences.

automotive-ev-battery.title: VW ID. Cross launches in Germany – with a starting price of €34,025
automotive-ev-battery.summary: First unveiled a week ago, the VW ID. Cross is now available for configuration in Germany. However, Volkswagen will initially only offer the electric crossover with a 155kW engine and a 52kWh battery in the Life and Style trims. Taking into account the current ID. purchase premium, prices start at €34,025.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BC Hydro targets 3,500 public charging ports by 2035
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BC Hydro will invest C$700 million to expand its public charging network to 3,500 ports by 2035, more than tripling its current size. The rollout includes larger charging hubs, 200 kW chargers as the new standard and selected 400 kW ultra-fast charging sites.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EU: 270,000 electric cars registered in June
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Electric car registrations increased significantly across the EU in June: 61 per cent more EVs hit the road compared to the same month last year. The electric car market also gained noticeable momentum in the first half of the year overall.

automotive-ev-battery.title: ‘The electric truck is no longer a technology problem’ – Professor Markus Lienkamp on charging electric trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The battery may be installed in the truck, but successful electrification depends on much more than the vehicle itself. In this in-depth interview, TUM Professor Markus Lienkamp explains why grid connections, depot strategies and megawatt charging will determine the market ramp-up of electric trucks – and which mistakes industry and policymakers should avoid.

automotive-ev-battery.title: India: BillionE to deploy over 150 heavy-duty electric trucks for JK Cement
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Indian e-mobility-as-a-service (eMaaS) platform BillionE has secured a contract to deploy over 150 heavy-duty electric trucks for JK Cement. It has already commissioned 20 units from this agreement as a pilot project.

finance-markets.title: US hits 60 countries with new duties as Trump rebuilds tariff wall

finance-markets.title: Asian Stocks Fall on Tech, Brent Trades Above $100: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Asian stocks followed Wall Street lower as investors grew skeptical that the billions being poured into the artificial intelligence buildout will generate adequate returns. Brent traded above $100 a barrel.

finance-markets.title: Yen’s Worst Week Since May Brings It Close to 165 Versus Dollar
finance-markets.summary: The yen is on track for its biggest weekly loss in more than two months, with intervention warnings doing little to deter investors betting on further weakness.

science-space.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
science-space.summary: Continuing agency efforts to bring space closer to home, NASA+ is heading to more streaming platforms. On Thursday, NASA announced its programming is on Fire TV Channels. Fire TV customers can easily access this content by asking Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future programming on Fire TV may include science mission launches, a test flight for […]

science-space.title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
science-space.summary: Crews at NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans transport the 130-foot-tall Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank out of a production cell inside the main factory building into a detached test building on a separate portion of the 829-acre site on May 15, 2026. The liquid hydrogen tank will form part of the core stage for […]

science-space.title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars
science-space.summary: NASA MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution) mission scientists have uncovered a key puzzle piece in understanding certain types of auroras on Mars, finding that they form in a similar way to Earth-based auroras. Results published Thursday in Nature Communications show the same mechanism that circulates and catapults charged particles into Earth’s atmosphere is happening at […]

science-space.title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Closes Out Space Station Mission
science-space.summary: After eight months aboard the International Space Station for his first mission, NASA astronaut Chris Williams is preparing to return to Earth. During his assignment, Williams contributed to research for new cancer treatments, advanced the production of materials to improve computers and electronics, ventured into the vacuum of space to complete two spacewalks, and much […]

science-space.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
science-space.summary: Snow-capped mountains carved by deep river valleys preside over northwestern Washington state.

science-space.title: Vulcan Centaur swapped out again: NASA goes with SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket for launch of space weather mission
science-space.summary: NASA's SunRISE mission has been assigned a new launch vehicle: It will now be delivered to orbit by a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket rather than a United Launch Alliance Vulcan Centaur.

science-space.title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4: Release date & how to watch Paramount+’s sensational sci-fi series
science-space.summary: 'We've seen a lot of strange new worlds, but we’ll never see something like this again'

science-space.title: Scientists may have finally solved the mystery of the sun's missing silver
science-space.summary: Scientists say the sun has a little more silver than we thought.

science-space.title: How much better is the Canon EOS R6 III than previous models for astrophotography?
science-space.summary: The latest edition of the Canon EOS R6 III adds 8 MP in resolution compared to the EOS R6 II, a CFexpress card slot and improved low-light autofocusing capability but do these upgrades actually benefit the astrophotographer?

science-space.title: NASA's retired astronauts are hitting the road to get young people to vote: 'one small step for you, and one giant leap for our country.'
science-space.summary: "We have to do what we can to ensure that the country makes it through the challenges that we're dealing with here, and that is the message that we're going to be taking to colleges and universities."

health-biotech.title: STAT+: In win for RFK Jr., FDA advisory panel narrowly votes to allow compounding of unapproved peptides
health-biotech.summary: The panel recommended allowing compounding pharmacies to produce popular but unproven BPC-157 and KPV peptides.

health-biotech.title: U.S. health officials are investigating a new outbreak of a diarrhea-causing parasite
health-biotech.summary: Federal health officials are investigating a new outbreak involving the diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: European regulators take steps to bolster clinical trial transparency
health-biotech.summary: The European Medicines Agency is taking what advocacy groups are calling unexpectedly “rapid action” to ensure that clinical trial results are made public as required by law.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Popular insulin product enters shortage as other pens leave market
health-biotech.summary: The Lantus Solostar insulin pen isn't in official FDA shortage, but patients are having a tough time finding it.

health-biotech.title: Senate health panel delays vote on CDC nominee
health-biotech.summary: Too few senators were in attendance to proceed with a vote on the CDC director nominee.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: What’s in the last-minute flurry of health care bills?
health-biotech.summary: Congress is scrambling to advance a slew of health care bills before an August recess. Price transparency and prior authorization top the list.

health-biotech.summary: Today's biotech news features the FDA weighing the fate of peptides; approvals for RevMed, GSK; and data on Lilly's "triple-G."

health-biotech.summary: Sean Kaufman, nominated to run ASPR, has a track record of questioning the safety of vaccines.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Trump administration quietly picks Timothy Westlake to lead mental health agency
health-biotech.summary: Timothy Westlake, an emergency doctor nominated to head SAMHSA, has been active in addiction policy in Wisconsin.

crypto-web3.title: Clarity Act expected to miss its window before Congress' summer break, leadership says
crypto-web3.summary: The deadline to get the crypto market structure bill done before senators scatter will likely be missed, says Majority Leader Thune, though it may get a start before then.

crypto-web3.title: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X account hacked to promote token amid memecoin frenzy
crypto-web3.summary: The post, now deleted, touted a token called $VLAD and falsely claimed it would be listed on Robinhood.

crypto-web3.title: Tassat wants to help smaller banks tap the trillion-dollar stablecoin boom before Wall Street lock them out
crypto-web3.summary: The former Signet developer aims to launch early next year a marketplace connecting stablecoin issuers with regional lenders to manage reserves.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase's corporate customers can now accept payments from AI agents
crypto-web3.summary: Rolling out this week, Coinbase Business users will be able to accept payments from AI agents, done via the x402 protocol, developed and incubated by the exchange.

crypto-web3.title: The SEC settles with Coinbase over its missing Gary Gensler texts
crypto-web3.summary: A new court status report reveals the agency will pay a flat fee to resolve a two-year fight over what former Chair Gary Gensler knew about Ethereum.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto for Advisors: It’s time for tokenization to get to work
Uniswap pushes deeper into tokenized assets with permissioned trading pools
crypto-web3.summary: The framework, developed with Superstate, Securitize and Dowgo, lets regulated funds and securities trade on Uniswap while enforcing compliance rules necessary for institutions.

crypto-web3.title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs Clarity Act despite banking industry's concerns over stablecoin rules
crypto-web3.summary: The Goldman Sachs CEO said the crypto market structure bill would create a more stable regulatory framework, breaking with other major bank leaders who oppose key stablecoin provisions.

crypto-web3.title: The KIDS Act is way worse than digital carding — it is a mass surveillance system
crypto-web3.summary: The fact that lawmakers are even contemplating a bill that would create a surveillance and censorship regime should be a wake-up call for everyone who values privacy and free expression, argues Electronic Frontier Foundation's India McKinney.

crypto-web3.title: BlackRock, Coinbase, Strategy in a new group pledging $15 million to prepare Bitcoin for quantum threats
crypto-web3.summary: Members will direct funding independently, with the consortium taking no role in Bitcoin governance or protocol decisions.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.

cybersecurity.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
cybersecurity.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software wi

cybersecurity.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
cybersecurity.summary:
A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client.

The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email directory, the password saved in the browser and the codes kept for two-factor recovery. Opening the message was enough to start it.

The NSA, CISA and partner agencies published

cybersecurity.title: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
cybersecurity.summary:
Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful.

A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Other threats hid in open systems, weak code, and normal network traffic.


 The threats change every week. Subscribe, and we’ll alert you when each new ThreatsDay Bulletin is out.


The danger was

cybersecurity.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within which the agent runs to read or write files anywhere on the Mac.

Accomplish AI, which shared details of the vulnerability with The Hacker News ahead of publication, said about 500,000 macOS users running

cybersecurity.title: Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge
cybersecurity.summary:
The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine ahead of the encryptor.

The implant never opens an outbound connection of its own. Its process talks to 127.0.0.1 and nothing else. It starts Chrome or Edge in headless mode and drives the browser

cybersecurity.title: China-Nexus JadeProx Uses New TriBack Loader in Government and Healthcare Attacks
cybersecurity.summary:
An exposed Alibaba Cloud server has revealed a China-nexus operation that Group-IB tracks as JadeProx. The cluster has targeted government, healthcare, and education organizations across Asia and Latin America with a previously undocumented Windows loader called TriBack Loader.

Group-IB found the server in mid-April 2026 in Alibaba Cloud's Singapore region; it was offline by the time the report

cybersecurity.title: How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
cybersecurity.summary: Most people understand identity theft as an attacker stealing a real person's sensitive information and impersonating them. Synthetic identity fraud is much harder to catch. Instead of stealing a real identity, the attacker manufactures a new one, frankensteining together several real data points with fabricated ones to create a person who doesn't exist. Since no real victim monitors misuse, a

cybersecurity.title: Attackers Weaponize GitHub Actions Runners to Target cPanel and WHM Servers
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a large-scale campaign that has turned compromised GitHub repositories into distributed attack infrastructure designed to target cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) instances.

The activity involves malicious Packagist development versions spanning 10 packages associated with a legitimate PHP and DevOps developer, dinushchathurya, between July 12 and 13,

cybersecurity.title: Google Adds Selfie Video Recovery for Users Locked Out of Their Accounts
cybersecurity.summary:
Google on Thursday announced a new way for users to sign-in to their accounts by letting them take a selfie video.

The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing recovery methods to log in to an account, including an email address or a phone number. The idea is to use a video selfie as a way to regain access if a user ever gets locked out or doesn't have access

startups-vc.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
startups-vc.summary: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

startups-vc.title: Edtech platform raises $4.5M to help teach students how to vibe code
startups-vc.summary: Imagi announced a $4.5 million seed round, with investors including Brighteye Ventures, Day One Capital, and artist Will.i.am.

startups-vc.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
startups-vc.summary: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

startups-vc.summary: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

startups-vc.title: The Rise And Rise Of Billion-Dollar-Plus Rounds
startups-vc.summary: So far this year, 60% of global startup funding across stages — around $320 billion — went to rounds of $1 billion or more, per Crunchbase data, with such rounds instrumental in pushing global funding for the first half of the year to record levels.

startups-vc.title: Is On-Prem Making A Comeback?
startups-vc.summary: While cloud migration previously offered unmatched speed and cost efficiency, decision-makers now perceive localized systems as offering superior long-term security. In this guest commentary, strategic adviser Itay Sagie shares three trends he believes are pushing on-prem back into the limelight.

energy-climate.title: Hybrid PPAs see growing adoption in Europe despite a 24% premium over solar PPAs
energy-climate.summary:
Hybrid PPAs are gaining traction across Europe as developers pair solar projects with battery storage to counter price volatility and negative electricity prices. Although priced 24% higher than solar-only PPAs, hybrid contracts offer greater revenue potential and are becoming increasingly attractive to corporate buyers.
The post Hybrid PPAs see growing adoption in Europe despite a 24% premium over solar PPAs appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Southern Africa shifts toward competitive wholesale electricity markets
energy-climate.summary:
The latest in a series of articles on energy markets in Southern Africa explores the liberalization of the Southern African Power Pool and emergence of South Africa’s wholesale electricity market.

energy-climate.title: Hail storm damages PV systems across Italy’s Marche region
energy-climate.summary:
A severe hail storm in Italy's Marche region damaged several PV systems, with industry representatives reporting a rise in hail-related losses in recent years.
The post Hail storm damages PV systems across Italy’s Marche region appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Spanish operational PV assets stabilize at €596,000/MW following 50% value decline
energy-climate.summary:
Spain’s PV asset market is entering a stabilization phase after two years of valuation declines, with operational solar assets averaging €596,000/MW. Investors are increasingly favoring projects with regulated revenues, PPAs, and battery storage potential as merchant assets face pressure from falling capture prices.
The post Spanish operational PV assets stabilize at €596,000/MW following 50% value decline appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: ML System presents solar tile for demanding architectural applications
energy-climate.summary:
Poland-based ML System has launched PhotonRoof, a BIPV solar tile designed to combine photovoltaic generation with traditional roofing aesthetics. Available in three design lines and multiple finishes, the system targets residential, commercial, and heritage projects requiring high architectural integration.
The post ML System presents solar tile for demanding architectural applications appeared first on pv magazine Global.

energy-climate.title: Sinovoltaics updates solar module manufacturer financial stability ranking

energy-climate.title: Fox ESS launches high-voltage battery-inverter solution for C&I solar
energy-climate.summary:
Fox ESS’s Power Beast platform integrates H3 Plus hybrid inverters with CQ7 high-voltage battery stacks, featuring LFP chemistry, IP65 protection, up to 98.5% inverter efficiency, and more than 95% battery round-trip efficiency. The system supports PV arrays of up to 250 kW, includes three independent battery inputs, and provides EPS backup with transfer times of less than 10 ms.

asia-pacific.title: Why the divorces of China’s A-share firm owners provoke market nerves
asia-pacific.summary:
China’s A-share market has seen another high-profile divorce case result in a massive asset split, involving the transfer of 6 billion yuan (US$886 million) – the highest this year – and raising concerns over corporate governance stability and share price fluctuations.
Although the scope of the divorce settlement is not at all comparable to that of Jeff Bezos or Bill Gates, it has made tens of thousands of retail investors worry about their portfolio holdings and paper wealth.

asia-pacific.title: China targets new space-based computing network to process data born in orbit
asia-pacific.summary:
China has unveiled its latest ambitious space-based computing network designed to process satellite data in orbit using AI before sending the results to Earth.
Xingshu, or “Star Hub”, will eventually comprise around 1,000 satellites, according to Chi Nan, an optical communications professor at Fudan University.
The network is being jointly developed by Fudan and Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co.
It would offer on-demand, time-based computing services in orbit, allowing governments,.

asia-pacific.title: ‘Rats everywhere’: numbers caught are up 323%. So why are authorities not worried?
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong resident Yau Suk-ming was startled in her kitchen when her daughter turned on a countertop blender and a rat leapt out into the open.
The rodent scurried away before the 74-year-old retiree could catch it, but she was not surprised as she had earlier noticed rat droppings in her flat on Kowloon City’s Bulkeley Street.
“Turns out rats are afraid of ‘earthquakes’ – but I was not afraid, I am very proud of that,” she joked, saying she could not do much but put up with them.

asia-pacific.title: Why Japan’s fighter jet future looks ‘more optimistic’ with Canada’s entry
asia-pacific.summary:
Canada’s decision to take a role in the next-generation Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP) has eased Japan’s fears over its fighter jet future, while Germany is also reported to be exploring whether it could join the project that also involves Britain and Italy.
The moves could give fresh momentum to GCAP, a programme to develop a next-generation fighter that Japan sees as critical to replacing its ageing F-2 fleet and avoiding a gap in its air defences, analysts say.

asia-pacific.title: More Americans think China is world leader in AI, new survey reveals
asia-pacific.summary:
More than one-third of Americans see China, and not the United States, as the global leader in artificial intelligence (AI), according to a Pew Research Centre survey released on Thursday, as US President Donald Trump confirmed the technology would be on the agenda when his Chinese counterpart visits the US later this year.
The survey found that 36 per cent of Americans believe China is more advanced than the United States in developing artificial intelligence. By comparison, only 12 per cent...

asia-pacific.title: How scholar Ackbar Abbas changed the way people thought about Hong Kong
asia-pacific.summary:
Ackbar Abbas, a Hong Kong-born scholar whose influence extended beyond academia to shape artists, filmmakers, writers and many others, died on July 13 at the age of 84 after a brief battle with cancer.
Over decades of teaching at the University of Hong Kong (HKU) and the University of California, Irvine (UCI), Abbas did not simply analyse Hong Kong – he altered the way Hong Kong could be described.
His 1997 book Hong Kong: Culture and the Politics of Disappearance was particularly influential...

asia-pacific.title: Hongkongers increase cross-border shopping splurges, spending data shows
asia-pacific.summary:
Even as local retail spending remains resilient, Hong Kong consumers are shopping more across the border in mainland China, drawn by lower prices and wider adoption of digital payments, according to data from payment giant UnionPay International.
Offline UnionPay card spending in mainland China by Hong Kong residents rose nearly 30 per cent year on year in the first half of 2026, while online and digital transactions recorded even stronger growth, data released on Thursday showed.
The trend...

asia-pacific.title: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike, but ‘cockroach’ protests to continue
asia-pacific.summary:
Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday over leaked medical school entrance exam papers that protesters say enabled cheating, but demonstrations demanding the education minister’s resignation were set to continue in the biggest youth-led challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government.
Youth protesters, led by the self-named Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) movement, demand that Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resign over the leaks that affected some 2...

asia-pacific.title: Trump throws newly signed Saudi nuclear deal into doubt with Israel recognition demand
asia-pacific.summary:
The White House threw a US-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal into question on Thursday, a day after it was signed, emphasising that it depended on the oil-rich Gulf country recognising Israel under a 2020 diplomatic initiative.
“The Civil Nuclear Deal … will be approved, but is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords,” US President Donald Trump wrote on social media, adding that the deal would not include enrichment and involve only non-military use of..

asia-pacific.title: Why PLA fighter jet sorties near Taiwan dropped to a 3-year low in the first half of 2026
asia-pacific.summary:
There is a change in the skies and waters around Taiwan.
In the first half of this year, the number of People’s Liberation Army (PLA) fighter jets detected near the island more than halved year on year to 1,334.
It was a three-year low and marked the lowest operational tempo since 2023, according to a tally by the South China Morning Post based on data from Taiwan’s defence ministry.
At the same time, the number of days no PLA jets were detected by Taiwan hit a new high, and the PLA did not...
